title: Y-fix: Apply diagnosed Codex MCP wiring fix + integration test for [mcp_servers.fabric] block
scope: Site identified by TASK-006 diagnosis (likely one of: install.ts, config.ts, resolver.ts, toml.ts) + new integration test fixture
description: Apply the patch identified in TASK-006's `_codex-mcp-diagnosis.md`. Patch site depends on diagnosis outcome — could be install.ts orchestration filter, config.ts loop gating, resolver.ts detection branch, or toml.ts writer logic. Add an integration test that runs `fab install` against a fixture environment with `~/.codex/config.toml` present and asserts the post-install file contains a `[mcp_servers.fabric]` block with the expected `command` + `args` keys. CONDITIONAL EXECUTION: if TASK-006 sets `defer_y_fix=true` (>1 day complexity), this task becomes a no-op placeholder — log defer reason in commit message and move Bug Y to a separate hotfix rc; rc.17 still ships via TASK-008 without depending on this fix.

files.target: Site named in _codex-mcp-diagnosis.md root cause section
files.change: Apply patch per diagnosis proposed-fix; one file modified in the no-defer path; zero files modified in the defer path
Setup: temp HOME with ~/.codex/config.toml; run installMcpClients; assert file contains [mcp_servers.fabric] block with command + args

implementation: Read `_codex-mcp-diagnosis.md` to get the exact patch site + proposed change
BRANCH A (no-defer, complexity ≤1 day): apply the patch to the diagnosed file; verify locally with manual repro from TASK-006
BRANCH B (defer): create a minimal placeholder commit documenting the defer + linking the hotfix rc plan; SKIP test creation; SKIP file changes; this task converges with status='deferred'
BRANCH A continued: write integration test at packages/cli/test/integration/codex-mcp-install.test.ts using a temp-HOME pattern (process.env.HOME redirect + cleanup)

Test setup: write a minimal valid `~/.codex/config.toml` to temp dir, set HOME to temp, run `installMcpClients` programmatically, read back the toml file, assert `mcp_servers.fabric` table exists with command='node' (or whatever shape Fabric writes)
Run: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test` — new test must pass; existing tests must not regress
Manual end-to-end smoke: `fab install --target /tmp/fixture` against a real ~/.codex/config.toml and grep for `[mcp_servers.fabric]` in the file

reference.pattern: Diagnosis-driven minimal-patch fix + regression integration test
reference.examples: Look at existing CodexTOMLConfigWriter usage sites (uninstall.ts:412+ mirrors install) for the writer API shape; follow the same call pattern in the integration test

criteria: BRANCH A (no-defer): post-install, `~/.codex/config.toml` contains a `[mcp_servers.fabric]` table with `command` and `args` keys (verify via test assertion + manual cat)
BRANCH A: new integration test `packages/cli/test/integration/codex-mcp-install.test.ts` exists and passes
BRANCH A: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test` passes including the new integration test
BRANCH B (defer): commit message explicitly references the defer decision from TASK-006 and links to a hotfix issue or note; no test added; no production code changed
Test command: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -t 'codex-mcp-install'` exits 0 in BRANCH A; in BRANCH B, the test does not exist and TASK-008 release notes carry a known-issue line for Bug Y
Behavioral expectation (BRANCH A): a user with ~/.codex/config.toml present runs `fab install` and the Fabric MCP server appears in their Codex client immediately, without manual toml edits

rationale.chosen_approach: Apply diagnosed fix + add regression test in same commit; use conditional defer-branch to protect rc.17 ship date if complexity surprises
alternatives_considered: Apply fix without integration test (skip regression coverage)
Always defer to hotfix regardless of TASK-006 outcome
decision_factors: Bug Y was parked once already — adding regression test is mandatory to prevent re-park
Defer-branch protects ship date; hotfix rc is acceptable for an isolated MCP-wiring bug
rationale.tradeoffs: Two execution branches mean the task spec is more complex; mitigated by clear BRANCH A vs BRANCH B convergence criteria

manual_checks: BRANCH A: cat ~/.codex/config.toml after install — verify [mcp_servers.fabric] block present with correct command/args
BRANCH A: run `fab uninstall` — verify [mcp_servers.fabric] block is removed (uninstall path is a separate symmetry concern, just smoke check)
BRANCH B: confirm commit message references defer + hotfix plan
success_metrics: BRANCH A: 1 new integration test added, 0 existing tests regressed
BRANCH A: post-install ~/.codex/config.toml contains valid [mcp_servers.fabric] table
BRANCH B: defer documented; release notes carry known-issue entry

risks.description: Fix patch interacts unexpectedly with non-Codex clients (Claude Code CLI / Cursor / Claude Desktop) breaking their MCP install
risks.mitigation: Run full integration test suite for all 4 client writers before merge; smoke-test each client manually after fix lands
risks.fallback: Revert patch + escalate to hotfix rc with broader test coverage
